title: VisuAlgo — Visualizing Sorting Algorithms
description: Interactive visualizations for sorting, graph, and data structure algorithms. Essential for building intuition before algorithms exams — you can step through each operation.

title: The Missing Semester of Your CS Education — MIT
description: MIT's free course covering what CS programs don't usually teach: shell scripting, Git internals, editors, debugging, and security basics. Highly recommended for anyone who feels gaps in their practical knowledge.

title: CS50: Introduction to Computer Science — Free
description: Harvard's legendary intro CS course, completely free on edX. Best starting point for beginners or anyone wanting to fill fundamentals gaps. The problem sets are genuinely challenging.

title: Build Your Own Redis — CodeCrafters
description: Step-by-step guide to implementing Redis from scratch in your language of choice. Surprisingly effective at building systems programming intuition. The RESP protocol section alone is worth it.

title: Awesome Self-Hosted — GitHub
description: Massive curated list of software you can host yourself. Great starting point for homelab projects or finding privacy-respecting alternatives to common SaaS tools.

title: A Cartoon Intro to WebAssembly — Mozilla
description: Lin Clark's illustrated guide to how WebAssembly actually works. Best conceptual introduction I've found — explains compilation, the stack machine, and integration with JavaScript clearly.

title: Roadmap.sh — Developer Learning Paths
description: Community-built learning roadmaps for different developer roles: frontend, backend, DevOps, and more. Useful for identifying gaps in your knowledge or deciding what to learn next.

title: Excalidraw — Collaborative Whiteboard for Diagrams
description: Open-source virtual whiteboard with a hand-drawn style. Perfect for sketching system architecture diagrams, algorithm visualizations for group study, or just thinking through problems visually.
